instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
How to check PAN card status online?
The PAN (permanent account number) is one of the most essential documents for financial transactions in India. However, you may usually face two situations with your PAN. One when you have applied for a new PAN card, and two, when you have requested updates to your existing one. This is where keeping track of your application status becomes important to make sure there are no delays in receiving it.
Fortunately, checking your PAN card status has become quite simple today. All you need to do is follow a few easy steps online and receive your application status right at your fingertips. Let’s take a look!
Essential documents to check PAN card status online
Some of the most important documents required to check your PAN card status include:1
Coupon/Application number: This number will be sent to you when you submit your PAN application via the UTIITSL website.
Acknowledgement number: This number will be shared with you when you submit your application through the NSDL website.
Methods to check PAN card status online
There are several ways to check your PAN card status online. Let's explore each in detail!1
Via NSDL
Visit the official NSDL PAN status tracking page.
Select ‘PAN - New/Change Request’ right from the drop-down menu there.
Enter your acknowledgement number.
Next up, enter the captcha code and click submit.
Your PAN status will be right there on the screen.
Via UTIITSL
Visit the UTIITSL PAN card status tracking page.
Here, enter your application coupon number/PAN number.
Enter your date of birth and captcha code.
Click submit.
Besides this, you can also check your PAN card status via:
Call 1
This is yet another one of the easiest ways. All you need to do is simply get in touch with the TIN call centre. This is:
020-27218080 (7:00 am to 11:00 pm: Monday-Sunday; 7 days)
08069708080 (24 hours)
Upon calling, you need to provide them with your 15-digit acknowledgement number, your DOB, name, and other necessary details. The executive will share details regarding your application status immediately.
SMS 1
This process will take a few minutes. All you need to do is send your acknowledgement number to 57575 in the following format: NSDLPAN<acknowledgement number>. Next, keep an eye on the mobile number you've added to your PAN application form. Your PAN status will be sent there.
WhatsApp 1
Open WhatsApp, and send ‘Hi’ to 8096078080.
Next, follow the chatbot instructions.
Provide the necessary details and do as requested.
Within seconds, you will receive your PAN status.
Common PAN card status messages
When you check your PAN status, you may come across one of these messages. These usually mean:
‘Under process’/ ‘Application is inwarded’: Your application is still being processed. 1
‘Rejected’: There was a potential issue with your application. This can be anything (incorrect documents, signature mismatch, etc.)3
‘Dispatched’: This indicates that your PAN card is on its way to get delivered to the given address.
‘No record found’: This means your details are incorrect. It could also mean that it's too soon, and your application may not have been processed yet.
Exceptions when online PAN tracking may not work
While online tracking is quite reliable, nothing comes without its exceptions. Hence, your tracking may not work due to:3
Technical issues on portals: At times, the NSDL or UTIITSL websites may face technical glitches. You can try again later.
Recently applied PAN cards: In case you've applied in the last 2-3 days, there are solid chances your record might not have been filed and updated yet. Here, you need to wait for a few days and check again.
Incorrect details: If you enter any inaccurate details, you will not get any results. Hence, make sure you double-check your details appropriately.
Offline applications: If you have applied via a physical form at a PAN centre, the tracking might take a little longer.
Conclusion
Tracking your PAN card status online has become quite quick and easy. If you do not have internet access, you can check the status via phone or SMS. However, if your status isn't updating, shows an error, or indicates that your PAN has been dispatched but not received, do not panic. Ensure that you enter accurate details to avoid potential errors. Double-check your details. Wait for a few days if necessary. If problems persist, reach out to the respective issuing authority for assistance.
FAQs
How long does it take to get a PAN card after applying?
It usually takes 15-20 business days for a new PAN card to be issued online.1 In case there are corrections, you can expect a 15-day timeline. 4
Can I track my PAN card status without an acknowledgement number?
Yes. You can track your PAN card status without an acknowledgement number via the TIN-NSDL (now Protean) portal. Enter your name, date of birth, and other details as requested. Click on submit and track your PAN status immediately. 1
What should I do if my PAN card status shows ‘dispatched’ but I haven't received it?
If the status shows ‘dispatched’, and you haven't received the card, make sure you check in with the courier tracking details (provided by the NSDL/UTIITSL). If it says dispatched, do not hesitate to raise/file a complaint on TIN-NSDL (https://www.protean-tinpan.com/). 2
Sources
https://cleartax.in/s/pan-card-status
https://cleartax.in/s/pan-card-customer-carehttps://
https://cleartax.in/s/pan-card-rejection
https://cleartax.in/s/how-to-update-or-correct-pan-card-details
Disclaimer:
The views expressed in this article is not to be construed as professional advice and users are advised to seek independent professional/expert advice before making any decisions based on the same. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in

through UTIITSL).
